hero.description: The Educational and Research Institute of Cybernetics, Information Technologies and Engineering — 25 years of building a digital future together, day by day, step by step, through education, research and the development of a scientific and educational ecosystem.

chapters.label: Origins
chapters.title: How it all began
paragraphs: The year 2000. A small department, a handful of enthusiasts, and one firm belief: information technology is the future, and Rivne would have its own specialists in the field. That is when the faculty was born — the one that would become NNI KITI.

Over more than a decade, student numbers grew, new specialisations appeared, and the faculty outgrew itself. In 2013 that progress was formally recognised — we became an educational and research institute.
With every passing year we changed, yet never betrayed our core mission: to train professionals who solve real-world problems. From applied mathematics — to artificial intelligence and cybersecurity.

chapters.label: Achievements
chapters.title: Our accomplishments
paragraphs: NNI KITI students represented Ukraine at the ACM/ICPC World Finals (2003) and international semi-finals in competitive programming (2011, 2012), winning prizes at KPI-Open and the All-Ukrainian Programming Olympiad.

Young scientists of the institute are laureates of the President of Ukraine Award and recipients of Cabinet of Ministers scholarships. Students completed semester exchanges in the UK, Germany and Poland through academic mobility programs.

Institute teams won the university startup competition Cool Idea, and graduates build successful careers at leading IT companies in Ukraine and worldwide. Over the past 5 years, faculty published more than 32 articles indexed in SCOPUS.

chapters.label: Today
chapters.title: NNI KITI
paragraphs: January 15, 2024 — the Educational and Research Institute of Automation, Cybernetics and Computing was renamed to the Educational and Research Institute of Cybernetics, Information Technologies and Engineering (NNI KITI).

Over this time the institute became a place where ideas matured, initiatives were born and personalities were shaped. It is a space where knowledge becomes action and aspirations become achievements. For 25 years we have been building a digital future together — every day, step by step, through education, research and the development of the scientific and educational ecosystem.

events.title: Department of Computational Mathematics
events.description: September 1966 — the Department of Computational Mathematics was established as a result of splitting the Department of Higher Mathematics. It became the foundation of the future Department of Computing Engineering.

events.title: Department of Information Systems
events.description: September 1, 1997 — the Department of Information Systems and Mathematical Modelling was established, headed by Dr. of Technical Sciences, Prof. Anatoliy Vlasiuk. It is the direct predecessor of the modern Department of Computer Sciences and Applied Mathematics.

events.title: Faculty established
events.description: August 25, 2000 — the Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Computer-Integrated Systems was established on the basis of the Department of Applied Mathematics and the Department of Electrical Engineering and Automation. Headed by Dr. of Technical Sciences, Prof. Anatoliy Pavlovych Vlasiuk.

events.title: Reorganised into institute
events.description: January 23, 2013 — the Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Computer-Integrated Systems was reorganised into the Educational and Research Institute of Automation, Electrical Engineering and Information Technologies.

events.title: Renamed to NNI AKOT
events.description: April 12, 2013 — renamed to the Educational and Research Institute of Automation, Cybernetics and Computing. Petro Oleksandrovych Tadeyev was appointed Director (2013–2020).

events.title: Department of Economic Cybernetics
events.description: In 2013 the Department of Economic Cybernetics was established on the basis of the Department of Applied Mathematics and the Department of Labour Resources and Entrepreneurship to train specialists in economic cybernetics.

events.title: Department of Computer Sciences
events.description: In 2016 the Department of Computer Sciences was established at the institute, headed by Dr. of Pedagogical Sciences, Prof. Tulashvili Yuriy. New programmes were launched: Information Systems and Technologies, and Professional Education (Computer Technologies).

events.title: Department CTEK
events.description: In 2019 the Department of Computer Technologies and Economic Cybernetics (CTEK) was formed by merging the Department of Computer Sciences into the Department of Economic Cybernetics. Its goal: training IT specialists who can solve real business problems.

events.title: New institute director
events.description: Petro Mykolayovych Martyniuk, Dr. of Technical Sciences, Professor, became the Director of the Educational and Research Institute of Cybernetics, Information Technologies and Engineering.

events.title: NNI KITI
events.description: January 15, 2024 — the Educational and Research Institute of Automation, Cybernetics and Computing was renamed to the Educational and Research Institute of Cybernetics, Information Technologies and Engineering (NNI KITI).

events.title: Information Security programme
events.description: In 2024 the Department of Computing Engineering launched a new educational programme Information Security within the speciality Cybersecurity and Information Protection.

events.title: Data Engineering programme
events.description: In 2025 the Department of Computer Sciences and Applied Mathematics launched the Data Engineering educational programme within the speciality Systems Analysis and Data Science.

chronicle.title: Read the full
chronicle.lead: From a single department to an institute shaping Ukraine's digital future. Twenty-five years, five directors, thousands of graduates.

chapters.label: Origins
chapters.title: How it all began
chapters.body:
The idea of uniting mathematics and computer technologies into a single educational and research space emerged long before the institute's official founding. In 1966, the university established the Department of Computational Mathematics — the first seed of what would later grow into NNI KITI. In 1997, the Department of Information Systems and Mathematical Modelling opened under the leadership of Anatoly Pavlovych Vlasiuk, laying the foundation of the modern CSAM department.

August 25, 2000 marks the date of birth: the Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Computer-Integrated Systems united the Department of Applied Mathematics and the Department of Electrical Engineering and Automation. The first dean was Anatoly Pavlovych Vlasiuk — a person who defined the spirit of the future institute by combining analytical rigour with openness to innovation.

On January 23, 2013, the faculty received institute status, and on April 12 of the same year it was renamed the Institute of Automation, Cybernetics and Computer Engineering. Petro Oleksandrovych Tadeyev became its first director.

chapters.label: Growth
chapters.title: Years of formation
chapters.body:
From 2013 to 2020 the institute actively built its educational ecosystem. In 2013 the Department of Economic Cybernetics was created, focusing on the intersection of IT and business. In 2016, the Department of Computer Sciences opened under the leadership of Yurii Yosypovych Tulashvili — along with it came the programmes 'Information Systems and Technologies' and 'Professional Education'.

2019 was marked by a structural merger: the Department of Computer Sciences joined the Department of Economic Cybernetics, forming CTEC — Computer Technologies and Economic Cybernetics. This allowed resources to be concentrated and gave students a wider range of applied competencies.

During these years the culture of the institute was shaped: scientific seminars, student project teams, and the first academic exchange agreements. The institute ceased to be merely a structural division of the university — it became a place where ideas are born.

chapters.label: Present
chapters.title: NNI KITI: a new chapter
chapters.body:
In 2020 Petro Mykolayovych Martyniuk, Doctor of Technical Sciences and Professor, took the helm — and a new stage began. The direction: openness to international partnerships, investment in laboratory infrastructure, and the launch of new educational programmes.

January 15, 2024 — official renaming. The Institute of Cybernetics, Information Technologies and Engineering — NNI KITI. The new name reflects reality: cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data engineering and IoT became key training directions.

In 2024–2025, new programmes were launched: 'Information Security' (CECE Department) and 'Data Engineering' (CSAM Department). Negotiations for partnerships with ETH Zurich and Warsaw University of Life Sciences are underway. 25 years is not a final full stop, but the starting point of the next chapter.

closing.description: Over 25 years, NNI KITI has built an environment where ideas matured, initiatives were born and personalities were shaped. This publication is not a conclusion, but a step forward.
closing.quote: "Dreams become plans. Plans become actions. And actions become the history we write together."

items.title: Computer Sciences
items.description: Fundamentals of programming, algorithms, data structures, computer architecture

items.title: Cybersecurity
items.description: Threat protection, cryptography, vulnerability analysis, ethical hacking

items.title: AI & Machine Learning
items.description: Artificial intelligence, neural networks, data processing, pattern recognition

items.title: Cloud & DevOps
items.description: Cloud platforms, containerisation, CI/CD, infrastructure as code

items.title: Web & Mobile
items.description: Web application development, mobile platforms, frontend, backend technologies

items.title: Computer Economics
items.description: Digital business, startups, entrepreneurship, IT project management
